name: Loop quantum gravity
summary: Quantises GR using Ashtekar's connection variables and a loop / spin-network basis. Predicts discrete eigenvalues for area and volume operators at the Planck scale. Background-independent and non-perturbative, in contrast to perturbative string theory; spin-foam dynamics provides a path-integral formulation. Active programs include LQC (loop quantum cosmology) for early-universe and black-hole singularity resolution.

addresses_puzzles.note: LQG must produce GR with a small Lambda in the semiclassical limit; specific predictions for Lambda are not derived from first principles. Listed for graph completeness as a candidate quantum-gravity framework.
Spin-network spectral dimension calculations (Modesto 2009) suggest reduction to d_s ~ 2 at Planck scales in LQG, though full derivation requires settling the dynamics.

Background-independent canonical quantisation of GR via Ashtekar connection variables and spin-network basis; claims a complete kinematical and (in spin-foam form) dynamical framework for QG (Ashtekar 1986; Rovelli-Smolin 1995; Rovelli-Vidotto 2014).

touches_benchmarks.note: Some LQG-inspired phenomenology predicts modified dispersion relations at Planck-scale energies; constraints from GRB and SME bounds are increasingly tight.

new_symmetries: SU(2) gauge symmetry of Ashtekar connection variables
introduces.note: Immirzi parameter (dimensionless O(1) coefficient setting the area gap).

history: Ashtekar (1986), 'New variables for classical and quantum gravity', PRL 57, 2244 — connection variables. Rovelli, Smolin (1990), 'Loop space representation of quantum general relativity', Nucl. Phys. B 331, 80. Spin-foam dynamics (Reisenberger-Rovelli, Engle-Pereira-Rovelli-Livine through 2000s-2010s).

references.citation: Ashtekar, 'New variables for classical and quantum gravity', Phys. Rev. Lett. 57, 2244 (1986)
Rovelli and Smolin, 'Loop space representation of quantum general relativity', Nucl. Phys. B 331, 80 (1990)
